mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer demand and construction activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive movements in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60d returns in greater than 50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of disinflation where CPIAUCSL growth decelerates below 0.3% monthly, equity markets price in lower future rate hikes, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in less than 45% of disinflationary months, or the signal reverses direction when inflation expectations remain anchored above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening corporate output and capacity utilization, which directly supports earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of months where INDPRO increases, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment gain exceeding 150 thousand signals robust labor market expansion and rising aggregate demand, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 150 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in greater than 60% of months with payroll gains above 150k, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for seasonal adjustments.

mechanism_narrative: When federal funds rates rise while the unemployment rate simultaneously increases, the combination signals stagflationary pressure and reduced corporate profitability, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60d returns in greater than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in multivariate models.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of robust economic expansion where real GDP growth exceeds 2.0% quarterly, corporate earnings accelerate and equity risk premiums compress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of quarters with GDP growth above 2.0%, or the predictive relationship breaks down when oil prices spike above 70 per barrel.
